title: PROPERTY MANAGEMENT - 211info
text: HOME FORWARD PROPERTY MANAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Maintains public housing units for low-income residents in which residents pay an affordable percentage of their income as rent. 135 SW Ash Street Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Eligibility varies by community. However, generally to be eligible: -Household income must not exceed the area median income percentage for the household size -At least one person in the household must be a citizen or permanent resident of the United States -Household size must meet occupancy guidelines. One bedroom is allowed for the head of the household and co-head/spouse, and one bedroom is allowed for every two people after that, regardless of age or gender -Applicants can apply for all waitlists they qualify for, regardless of whether they previously applied during other openings Priority status granted to: -Individuals who have a terminal illness and are diagnosed by a medical provider with a life expectancy of 12 months or less -Individuals with a disability who require housing with access features such as wheelchair accommodations and individuals eligible for in-home Medicaid services that require assistance with meals, bathing, laundry, and housekeeping to stop or delay premature entry into a nursing facility or adult foster care Hours: Office Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm, by appointment Email (503) 802-8300 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 802-8300 Property Management (503) 280-3750 Intake (503) 802-8405 Priority Status Message Phone (503) 280-3760 Public Housing Hotline Recording Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website for information on public housing availability. For most units, applications are only accepted when a community is added to the open waiting list. The estimated wait time can range from a year to several years. FEES: Rents vary by apartment and program type. Rent amounts in public housing are set at approximately 28.5%-31% of the household's monthly income. Rents at apartment communities that are not subsidized public housing generally are set below fair market rates. DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Multnomah County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet buses and MAX train lines stop 2-4 blocks from location Services: Housing Complaints * Public Housing Residents Public Housing Subsidized Housing Administrative Organizations Other Services Offered At This Location: HOUSING/SHELTER - AFFORDABLE HOUSING APARTMENT COMMUNITIES HOUSING/SHELTER - SECTION 8 HOUSING CHOICE VOUCHER PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. HOME FORWARD
